summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a blistering ’69 set, highlighted by an incendiary The Eleven and monster Alligator > Caution sequence.
review: The recording is a high‑fidelity soundboard source that most listeners describe as crisp, clear and with excellent instrument separation (aside from occasional oversaturation). Musically the show is legendary – the opening Dark Star → St. Stephen → The Eleven surge sets a volcanic pace, and the Alligator > Caution/Feedback jam cements it as one of ’69’s finest performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed five‑star reviews praise both the performance and audio, with only minor recording quirks noted; the consensus is that this is an outstanding show captured in excellent soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally described as crystal‑clear and well‑balanced with superb drum separation; a few reviewers note slight oversaturation or muffled spots but nothing that detracts from enjoyment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The opening trio (Dark Star → St. Stephen → The Eleven) delivers classic ’69 energy, and the second half builds to a fiery Alligator‑Caution climax; reviewers note the set feels cohesive despite a few minor dips after the high‑octane early jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly called ‘red‑hot’, ‘roaring’, and ‘on fire’; his solo work on The Eleven and Alligator is singled out as extraordinary.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh receives praise for “harmonic‑geometric genius” and being “in all the right places” throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboardists are noted for creating a low‑A feedback cello‑like texture that fuels the Caution/Feedback jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Listeners comment on clear drum separation, describing the rhythm section as tight and powerful.
